lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The patient's name is written on the board as John Doe, pending ID. Forty-something, blunt chest trauma, two fractured ribs confirmed by palpation, a third still in question. His blood pressure is doing something nobody likes. Maeve is on his left. Dana is on his right. They have not looked at each other since Bay One.

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: BP?

lines.speaker: Dana Voss
lines.text: Eighty-four over sixty and dropping. He lost pressure in the rig.

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Chest tube. Left side.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Dana is already reaching for the tray. Not because Maeve asked. Because she knew the ask was coming three seconds before it arrived. Theo watches from the doorway, a bag of saline in each hand, and does not move.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: Do you need me to—

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Stay where you are.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Dana preps the site. Her hands do not hesitate. She has done this in county hospitals, in fire-lit car wrecks on the M8, in a field once with a torch held in her teeth. She has done it beside Maeve Callahan. The body remembers what the mind has spent three years trying to forget.

lines.speaker: Dana Voss
lines.text: Ready.

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Go.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The insertion is clean. The sound that follows — air releasing — is the sound of something relieved. The patient's oxygen saturation climbs. Neither woman marks it with a word. The number on the monitor does the talking.

lines.speaker: Reza Shirazi
lines.text: His pressure is coming back. Eighty-nine.

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Keep watching it. If it drops again we have a bleeder somewhere we haven't found yet.

lines.speaker: Reza Shirazi
lines.text: Understood.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza has been in this bay for six months. He has seen Maeve work with three different trauma partners. He has never seen this. The efficiency here is not the efficiency of two skilled people occupying the same space. It is the efficiency of a single organism that has been cut in half and somehow kept moving on both sides.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: Saline's up.

lines.speaker: Dana Voss
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo does not say what he is thinking. What he is thinking is that he grew up watching his mother and his aunt move around each other in a kitchen after a falling-out that lasted eleven years, and it looked exactly like this. Purposeful. Contained. A grief so practiced it had become indistinguishable from competence.

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Secondary survey. Start at the head.

lines.speaker: Dana Voss
lines.text: Already there.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She is. She started before the instruction. Maeve clocks it and says nothing. There is a specific kind of pain in being known that precisely by someone you are no longer allowed to know back. She files it away in the same place she has been filing things for three years and keeps her hands moving.

lines.speaker: Reza Shirazi
lines.text: Ninety-three. He's stabilising.

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Don't say that yet.

lines.speaker: Dana Voss
lines.text: She's right. Wait for the repeat ultrasound.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Reza looks between them. He opens his mouth and closes it again. There is a language being spoken in this bay that he has not been taught. He is not sure he wants to learn it.

lines.speaker: Theo Okafor
lines.text: Ultrasound's coming in from Two. Sixty seconds.

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The monitor keeps its rhythm. The patient breathes. The bay smells of antiseptic and something colder underneath, the ambient temperature of two people holding a shared history at exactly arm's length. Dana straightens. Adjusts the tube. Checks the seal. Her movements are small and exact. Maeve watches her hands the way you watch something you recognise but are not supposed to claim.

lines.speaker: Dana Voss
lines.text: Seal's good. No leak.

lines.speaker: Maeve Callahan
lines.text: Confirmed.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: That is all. That is the whole of it. The patient does not know what is happening in the room around him. The monitor does not know. Only the four people in this bay know, and only two of them know why, and neither of those two will be explaining it tonight.
